audit_items.proof_trace: In the RESTART_NOTAG branch (L173-175), getline() has already allocated `line` (getline returned != -1), but `return RESTART_NOTAG` is reached without any `free(line)` call — the pointer is neither stored in `c->line` nor explicitly freed, confirming a heap leak. The EOF branch (L205-208) is not a practical leak for clean EOF since `line` starts as NULL and getline makes no allocation; it is only a theoretical concern on mid-stream read errors.
audit_items.attack_scenario: A callback registered via restart_register calls restart_get_kv in a while(== RESTART_OK) loop; if the metadata file contains a T-line whose tag is not registered (e.g., written by a different server version), restart_get_kv returns RESTART_NOTAG and leaks the getline-allocated line buffer — the loop exits without abort, accumulating heap memory that is never reclaimed during the check phase.
